title: Mustard on Carpet
stainChemistry: Same turmeric pigment chemistry as mustard-cotton, on carpet. turmeric pigment's key rule: AVOID ALKALINE (red-shifts yellow to dark red); UV SUNLIGHT FADES turmeric pigment (supports removal). Carpet adds construction risk: pooled liquid soaks to backing + pad. The oil carrier (mustard oil) must come off before peroxide can reach the dye. Synthetic face yarn tolerates NSD + 3% H₂O₂; wool narrows the lane.
whyThisWorks: Scrape bulk. NSD via damp cloth strips the oil carrier. 3% H₂O₂ oxidizes turmeric pigment. Sunlight exposure on a portable rug accelerates the fade. For fitted carpet, repeated peroxide + fan-dry cycles are the substitute. Alkaline cleaners = permanent red shift. Never.
defaultAssumption: Mustard on carpet is difficult. Aged mustard on light carpet often plateaus at faint yellow even professionally. Set expectations honestly. Escalate earlier for wool.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Scrape any solid mustard off. NEVER apply baking soda / ammonia / OxiClean — turns yellow to dark red permanently. Blot the spot straight down.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD in cool water, damp cloth, weighted dry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply cold NSD via damp cloth. Strips oil carrier. Never pour. Weighted dry towel extraction between passes. 2–3 cycles.
3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop on turmeric pigment shadow. Dwell 10 min. Blot dry. Patch-test on colored carpet first.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Fan, direct sun if rug can move
spottingProtocol.instruction: For portable rugs: place damp in direct sunlight. Fitted carpet: fan-dry, plan to repeat peroxide + dry over 1–2 days.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD damp cloth, heavy dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: NSD-damp cloth. Weighted dry towel 30 min.
Aged mustard usually needs 2–3 peroxide + fade cycles over 1–2 days. Set honest expectation: may plateau at faint yellow.

steps: 2. Cold NSD damp cloth + weighted extract — strip oil carrier.
4. Sun exposure on portable rugs; fan + peroxide repeat on fitted.
6. Repeat peroxide + fade cycles. Honest plateau expectation.
warnings: NEVER alkaline on turmeric pigment — permanent red shift

homeSolutions.agent: DON'T Use Baking Soda or Ammonia
homeSolutions.instruction: Mustard's yellow is turmeric. If turmeric pigment touches ANYTHING alkaline — baking soda paste, ammonia cleaner, OxiClean — it turns from yellow to DARK RED, and that red form is nearly impossible to remove. Use only Dawn dish soap + hydrogen peroxide on mustard carpet. Nothing alkaline.
Use a dull butter knife to lift any solid mustard off the carpet pile. Don't rub — drives it into the backing.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn + Cool Water on a DAMP Cloth
homeSolutions.instruction: Dampen a white cloth with cool water + a drop of Dawn. Press on the spot, lift, rotate. Never pour on carpet. Repeat 2–3 cycles. Dawn strips the oil that carries the yellow pigment — without this step, peroxide can't reach the dye.
After each wet pass, press a thick DRY towel + heavy book on the spot for 20–30 min. Pulls moisture UP out of the pad.

Dab 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide on the stain via a damp cloth. Patch-test a hidden carpet area first — peroxide lightens carpet dye. If it's a portable rug, set it in direct sunlight damp for 1–2 hours. For fitted carpet, fan-dry and repeat peroxide every 30–60 min over a sunny afternoon.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia. Test-patch on colored carpet.

homeSolutions.instruction: Mustard is slow to fade. Budget 2–3 peroxide + dry cycles over a day or two. Aged mustard on light carpet often plateaus at a faint yellow even professionally.

Wool carpet + mustard = professional cleaner. Also escalate if patch test showed dye bleed, if ammonia was already applied (red shift), or two cycles plateau. Tell them: "Mustard on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + 3% H₂O₂ + sun. [No alkaline used / baking soda was applied before I knew / plateau at faint yellow]."

steps: 1. NO alkaline cleaners — baking soda / ammonia / OxiClean all shift turmeric pigment to permanent red.
3. Dawn + cool water on DAMP cloth — strip oil carrier. 2–3 cycles.
4. Weighted dry towel between wet passes.
5. 3% hydrogen peroxide + sunlight on portable rugs. Multiple cycles.
6. Set honest expectations — aged mustard often plateaus faint yellow.
7. Escalate for wool, patch-test failure, or plateau.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if ammonia-containing cleaner has been applied (red shift), wool carpet, patch-test failure, or 2–3 peroxide + sun cycles plateau.
warnings: NO alkaline — red shift is permanent
Skip peroxide on dyed / patterned carpet without patch test

neverDo: NEVER alkaline on mustard — permanent red shift
fiberSensitivities: Synthetic face yarn: tolerates NSD, 3% H₂O₂

escalation.when: Alkaline was applied (red shift), wool carpet, patch-test failure, or two peroxide + sun cycles plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Mustard on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + 3% H₂O₂ + sun. [No alkaline / baking soda applied / plateau]."

contextQuestions: Carpet fiber — nylon / olefin / polyester / wool?
Has any alkaline cleaner been applied?
Is the carpet a portable rug (can sun-dry) or fitted?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Same turmeric pigment rules as mustard-cotton — alkaline red-shift is absolute, UV + peroxide is the fade workhorse. Carpet constraints add pad-protection + patch-test discipline.
